evidence.title: PX-1 White House fact sheet on Venezuelan oil revenue and U.S. operation
evidence.source_description: White House fact sheet page, extracted via web.open on 2026-06-30.
evidence.relevance: Official U.S. source admitting oil-revenue custody, blockade, lethal-force authorization, and an armed forces operation against Maduro within the case window.

evidence.title: DX-1 Department of War article on Maduro capture as joint law-enforcement mission
evidence.source_description: Department of War/Pentagon News article by Matthew Olay, opened via web.open on 2026-06-30.
evidence.relevance: Official source on Operation Absolute Resolve showing both adverse facts (Caracas operation and transition comments) and defense facts (capture/extraction, criminal proceedings, joint military/law-enforcement mission, no U.S. casualties, anti-narcoterrorism context).

evidence.title: PX-3 PBS/AP timeline of U.S. escalation and Maduro capture
evidence.source_description: PBS NewsHour page carrying Associated Press timeline by Ben Finley, Konstantin Toropin, and Regina Garcia Cano; extracted via web.open/find on 2026-06-30.
evidence.relevance: Contemporaneous credible reporting on the U.S. buildup, strikes, first known operation on Venezuelan soil, Jan. 3 large-scale strike across Caracas, capture/removal of Maduro and Flores, and related military posture.

evidence.title: PX-4 Brookings analysis of Jan. 3 Maduro operation and legal limit
evidence.source_description: Brookings Institution analysis page, extracted via web.open on 2026-06-30.
evidence.relevance: Independent expert analysis confirming a Jan. 3 U.S. special operation in Caracas and preserving the adverse official claim that it was not an invasion or occupation.

evidence.title: DX-2 Lead IG Operation Southern Spear report extract on counter-narcotics mission
evidence.source_description: Lead Inspector General report to Congress on Operation Southern Spear, January 1-March 31 2026, opened via web.open PDF extraction on 2026-06-30.
evidence.relevance: Official oversight source characterizing Operation Southern Spear as a mission to disrupt narcotics flows, with boat strikes, deployments, and interdictions, supporting defense that the broader campaign was counter-narcotics rather than territorial occupation.

evidence.title: PX-2 White House release collecting Secretary Rubio statements
evidence.source_description: White House release page, extracted via web.open on 2026-06-30.
evidence.relevance: Official U.S. statements acknowledging two-hour U.S. ground presence in Venezuela, oil quarantine/pressure, retained options, and the adverse claim that the action was not an invasion.

evidence.title: PX-5 Chatham House analysis of U.S. capture, strikes, and transition control
evidence.source_description: Chatham House expert comment by Marc Weller, extracted via web.open/find on 2026-06-30.
evidence.relevance: Independent international-law analysis characterizing the operation as U.S. forces operating in Venezuela, strikes around Caracas, special-forces abduction, and a U.S. announcement to run/administer a transition under threat of force.
